preview_text:
The model rates Belgium as the favorite with a 44% win probability, while the USA's chance stands at 26%. This represents a meaningful gap, with the underdog facing a substantial climb to victory on a neutral ground.

The expected distribution suggests a competitive match tilted toward Belgium, but draws remain plausible at 31%. The 17-percentage-point margin between Belgium's win probability and the USA's indicates the model does not expect a closely matched contest, though the relatively high draw probability implies the teams are not far apart in overall strength.

The model and market diverge noticeably on the USA's chances. Sportsbooks rate American victory at 35%, nearly 9 percentage points higher than the model's 26%. Market and model align more closely on Belgium (43% vs 36%) and draws (31% vs 29%). The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more reliable; the gap may reflect different information sets or risk preferences among traders versus the statistical model.
